title: Improvements relating to the stacking and reclaiming of bulk materials
abstract: The present disclosure relates to equipment for handling bulk materials. In a particular form, the present disclosure relates to equipment for use in stockpiling grains in a bulk storage system of the type commonly referred to as a bunker, although its application is not so limited. In one aspect, there is provided a mobile stacker and reclaimer combination comprising a mobile stacker, and a mobile reclaimer which is movable independently to the mobile stacker, wherein in use, the mobile reclaimer simultaneously collects a bulk material and feeds this to the mobile stacker.
claims: Claims 1. A mobile stacker and reclaimer combination comprising a mobile stacker, and a mobile reclaimer comprising a collector for a bulk material, and a power train so that the mobile reclaimer is movable independently to the mobile stacker, and wherein in use, the mobile reclaimer simultaneously collects the bulk material and feeds this to the mobile stacker. 2. The mobile stacker and reclaimer combination of claim 1 , wherein the mobile reclaimer comprises a base riding on a conveyance means, an elevator and a discharge chute for the bulk material, and wherein in use, the bulk material is collected in the collector at or near a ground level, and then conveyed to the discharge chute at an elevated position by the elevator. 3. The mobile stacker and reclaimer combination as in either of claims 1 or 2, wherein the collector comprises at least one elongate trough suspended from the base, the or each trough comprising an outlet port. 4. The mobile stacker and reclaimer combination of claim 3, wherein the or each trough further comprises at least one powered auger driving bulk material collected in the trough toward the trough outlet port. 5. The mobile stacker and reclaimer combination as in either of claims 3 or 4, wherein the collector comprises a pair of the elongate troughs arranged end to end alongside of each other, each trough comprising a proximal end depending from the base and comprising the trough outlet port, and at least one powered auger driving bulk material collected in the trough from at or toward a distal end toward its trough outlet port. 6. The mobile stacker and reclaimer combination as in any one of claims 2 through 5, wherein the elevator comprises a bucket elevator. 7. The mobile stacker of claim 6, wherein the reclaimer further comprises a conveyor boom slewably mounted atop of a tower which extends up from the base and receives bulk material from the bucket elevator and discharges this via the discharge chute. 8. The mobile stacker and reclaimer combination as in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the mobile stacker comprises a base riding on a conveyance means, a conveyor boom and a hopper depending from the base, and wherein in use, the conveyor boom is fed the bulk material via the hopper, and discharges the bulk material to a stockpile. 9. The mobile stacker and reclaimer combination as in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the mobile stacker comprises a connection means via which the mobile reclaimer is detachably securable with respect to the mobile stacker. 10. The mobile stacker and reclaimer combination as in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the mobile reclaimer comprises a connection means via which the mobile reclaimer is detachably securable with respect to the mobile stacker. 1 1. The mobile stacker and reclaimer combination as in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the conveyance means comprises a plurality of wheels. 12. The mobile stacker and reclaimer combination as in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the conveyance means comprises a plurality of crawler tracks. 13. A mobile reclaimer for use with a mobile stacker, the mobile reclaimer comprising a collector for a bulk material and a power train, so that the mobile reclaimer is movable independently to the mobile stacker, and wherein the mobile reclaimer is adapted to simultaneously collect the bulk material and feed this to the mobile stacker. 14. The mobile reclaimer of claim 13, wherein the mobile reclaimer comprises a base riding on a conveyance means, an elevator and a discharge chute for bulk material, and wherein in use, the bulk material is collected in the collector at or near a ground level and then conveyed to the discharge chute at an elevated position by the elevator. 15. The mobile reclaimer as in either of claims 13 or 14, wherein the collector comprises at least one elongate trough suspended from the base, the or each trough comprising an outlet port. 16. The mobile reclaimer of claim 15, wherein the or each trough further comprises at least one powered auger driving bulk material collected in the trough toward the trough outlet port. 17. The mobile reclaimer as in either of claims 15 or 16, wherein the collector comprises a pair of the elongate troughs arranged end to end alongside of each other, each trough comprising a proximal end depending from the base and comprising the trough outlet port, and at least one powered auger driving bulk material collected in the trough from at or toward a distal end toward its trough outlet port. 18. The mobile reclaimer as in any one of claims 14 through 17, wherein the elevator for bulk material comprises a bucket elevator. 19. The mobile reclaimer of claim 18, wherein the reclaimer further comprises a conveyor boom slewably mounted atop of a tower which extends up from the base and received bulk material from the bucket elevator and discharges this via the discharge chute. 20. A mobile stacker and reclaimer combination comprising a mobile stacker, and an adapter which is detachably securable with respect to the mobile stacker to convert the mobile stacker into a mobile reclaimer. 21. An adaptor adapted for detachable securement with respect to a mobile stacker to convert the mobile stacker into a mobile reclaimer. 22. A mobile stacker comprising a connection means via which an adapter is detachably securable with respect thereto, to convert the mobile stacker into a mobile reclaimer.
